headline: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

content.title: Asian stocks drop again as rollercoaster week draws to close
content.summary: Asian tech stocks sank again Friday as the rollercoaster ride that has characterised the week continued into the weekend, while crude prices edged back down after a brief rally sparked by news of an attack on a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.Oil prices resumed the downward shift that has marked the week, having risen around two percent Thursday on news that a cargo ship was damaged by an unknown projectile off Oman's coast in Hormuz.

headline: World shares skid as traders sell to lock in profits after recent rallies driven by AI
summary: World shares retreated Friday, led by heavy losses in Japan and South Korea as traders sold to lock in gains from recent rallies in stocks related to artificial intelligence.  Britain's FTSE 100 shed 0.4% to 10,490.62.  The wide swings in Tokyo and Seoul are typical of recent volatility in markets as investors react to the deluge of dollars heading into AI data centers  and other investments.

headline: Micron Stock Rallies To New Heights But A Warning Signal Lurks
summary: Micron Technology has asserted its top spot in the IBD 50 list of elite growth stocks with its blowout financial update, underscoring hot demand for its memory chips in the artificial intelligence boom.  During Thursday's session, Micron stock gapped up nearly 20% to an all-time high 1,255, breaking its previous pattern.  Micron's third-quarter report included a 1,215% jump in adjusted earnings and fourth-quarter projections that outstripped Wall Street's views.

headline: The Week in Numbers: Micron climbs high, Apple raises prices
summary:
STORY: From Micron's mega market cap to Apple's price hikes...

...this is the Week in Numbers.

:: The Week in Numbers

:: $1.4 trillion.

About $1.4 trillion was Micron's market cap after it briefly blew past the value of Meta and Tesla for the first time.

It came after the firm forecast quarterly profit and revenue well above expectations.

Micron is a major memory-chip maker and a key supplier to AI titan Nvidia.

The company's shares have more than tripled in value so far this year, and soared close to a fifth following its results.

:: 5%

Almost 5% is how far Apple shares fell after it announced price hikes.

The tech giant said its cheapest laptop will jump from $599 to $699, with iPad prices also rising - although the iPhone won't jump for now.

Apple says it can no longer avoid passing on rising costs to consumers.

The price of memory chips jumped by around 98% in the first quarter, and has kept soaring due to the AI boom.

:: 42% 

42% was how much shares of Wendy's soared as retail traders bought up the beaten-down stock in the latest meme-like rally.

Trading on the heavily shorted stock was paused multiple times due to volatility..

Shares briefly hit a more than seven-month high.

The fast food chain is battling weak sales and pressure from an activist investor, with its stock down more than 78% from record highs five years ago.

:: $648 billion

Almost $648 billion is how much Samsung plans to invest in South Korea over 10 years.

That's according to the Maeil Business Newspaper, which said the firm will announce its intentions on Monday.

The investment includes a potential $194 billion to build chip factories in the country's southwest, as well as plans for AI data centers, batteries and displays.

There was no immediate response to a request for comment from Samsung.

:: $606.5 million

And $606.5 million was H&M's operating profit during its fiscal second quarter.

That was smaller than expected for the Swedish fashion retailer.

H&M blamed weaker consumer demand and inflation made worse by the Iran war.

CEO Daniel Erver said Europe, particularly the western part of it, had gone through a difficult quarter.

headline: Wall Street follows global markets lower as traders sell to lock in profits after recent AI rallies
summary: Wall Street pointed toward losses before the opening bell Friday as volatility continued for chipmakers and other companies heavily invested in artificial intelligence.  Nasdaq futures tumbled another 1.4% and are on track for a more than 5% loss this week, the second such wipeout this month.  In premarket trading Friday, Micron fell 5%, Intel was down 3.6% and Broadcom and Qualcomm both retreated close to 2%.

content.title: Wall Street follows global markets lower as traders sell to lock in profits after recent AI rallies
content.summary: Wall Street pointed toward losses before the opening bell Friday as volatility continued for chipmakers and other companies heavily invested in artificial intelligence.  Nasdaq futures tumbled another 1.4% and are on track for a more than 5% loss this week, the second such wipeout this month.  In premarket trading Friday, Micron fell 5%, Intel was down 3.6% and Broadcom and Qualcomm both retreated close to 2%.

headline: Tech stocks sell-off, but the momentum trade is just 'taking a breather'
summary: A renewed sell-off in the tech sector started, stopped, and has now restarted to end this trading week. All three of the major indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are seeing red on Friday morning as Micron Technology (MU) even sheds its massive gains after releasing earnings on Wednesday. Opening Bid Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley, Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré, and PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati to discuss the state of the current momentum trade inside Big Tech stocks.

content.title: Tech stocks sell-off, but the momentum trade is just 'taking a breather'

A renewed sell-off in the tech sector started, stopped, and has now restarted to end this trading week. All three of the major indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are seeing red on Friday morning as Micron Technology (MU) even sheds its massive gains after releasing earnings on Wednesday.

Opening Bid Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley, Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré, and PNC Asset Management Group CIO Amanda Agati to discuss the state of the current momentum trade inside Big Tech stocks.
